name: Layered Cashew & Mushroom Loaf
recipeIngredient: Oil or butter to grease pan
recipeInstructions:
Note: Parsnips should be cooked and mashed with some margarine. (Use potatoes in place of parsnips when parsnips are not in season.)  If using fresh herbs, double the amount.
1. Heat oil and fry onion and garlic until soft.
2. Grind cashews in a nut mill, blender, or mincer, then mix with breadcrumbs.
3. Beat egg well and add to dry ingredients.
4. Mix in parsnips (or potatoes) and herbs. Add fried onion and garlic. Scrape in all the juice as the loaf can get slightly dry during baking.
5. Dissolve yeast in stock and add to other ingredients. Season well.
6. Melt margarine in a skillet and sauté mushrooms until soft. Grease a 2-pound loaf pan, then press half of the loaf mixture into the pan.
7. Cover with mushrooms and top with the remaining nut mixture. Press down firmly.
8. Cover with aluminum foil and bake at 350°F (180°C) for 1 hour or gas mark 4.
9. When cooked, let stand for 10 minutes before turning onto a plate. Slice and serve. Excellent hot or cold. It also freezes well.
10. To freeze, slice into portions, wrap in foil, and store in the freezer. To re-use, thaw, then heat in a medium oven until hot.
Serves 6 to 8
